summary: Based primarily on general principles about immune responses elicited by vaccinations rather than direct evidence within provided references, an effective anti-caries vaccine likely involves inducing high levels of **immunoglobulin G (IgG)** due its prolonged presence providing ongoing protection after initial exposure or inoculation.
